game_name: Anno 1602: Creation of a New World

danger_signals.visual_form: Declining population numbers in colony UI
danger_signals.meaning: Unmet citizen needs leading to emigration and economic threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual disturbance in settlement, population unhappiness display
danger_signals.meaning: Critical failure to meet demands—immediate economic and social collapse risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Negative or zero tax revenue in financial UI
danger_signals.meaning: Economic instability due to unmet citizen demands

danger_signals.visual_form: Musketeers, cannons, warships rendered on map
danger_signals.meaning: Active military threat or defensive positioning by factions

safety_signals.visual_form: Housing upgrades from Pioneers → Settlers → Citizens → Merchants
safety_signals.meaning: Successful economic development and satisfied population needs
Stable economic network reducing local resource scarcity

safety_signals.visual_form: Functioning buildings and goods flow visualization
New colonized islands visible on archipelago map
safety_signals.meaning: Successful exploration and resource acquisition security

faction_indicators.color_identity: One of four selectable colored banners (player-chosen)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Military units display faction color to distinguish player from AI factions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Colony structures and settlements marked with player color; ships display faction banner

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct visual appearance separating them from European settlements
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tribal settlements occupy specific islands; distinct architectural style
Trade outposts and neutral zones on map

faction_indicators.color_identity: Distinct banner colors separate from player
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Colonies, military units, and ships display unique faction colors for each AI opponent
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Settlement clusters and fortifications in rival territories; color-coded structures

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Small primitive dwellings, basic resource extraction buildings, minimal infrastructure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Limited building options available; low population density; slow resource generation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Upgraded housing structures, diverse building types, visible trade routes, multiple production chains
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Access to more sophisticated buildings unlocked; population demands cloth, alcohol, tobacco; increased tax revenue

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: High-quality housing, extensive infrastructure, spice production facilities, complex multi-island trade network
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Highest tier buildings accessible; maximum citizen demands; significant tax income flow

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visible weapon factories, military buildings, stationed units (musketeers, cannons, warships on docks)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Requires population threshold before military buildings unlocked; constant monetary upkeep for military structures

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Abandoned buildings, reduced construction, visible population decline in UI counters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Population riots triggered; tax income stops; emigration begins; visual decay of colony infrastructure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Trade-focused: merchant vessels and trading posts visible; Military-focused: fortifications and armed units stationed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Economic path: resource chains and trade routes emphasized; Military path: conquest infrastructure built

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fog of war obscuring unexplored archipelago islands; scout ships and initial landing structures visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Limited visibility into rival territories; gradual map revelation as colonies expand

environmental_storytelling.technique: Island size and resource availability variability
environmental_storytelling.example: Larger islands support more developed economies; smaller islands force specialization and trade dependency, visually reflecting population density and building density differences
Scattered islands naturally communicate the need for naval power and trade route establishment; island clustering suggests potential ally/enemy proximity
Visual upgrade from simple Pioneer huts → sturdy Settler homes → elegant Citizen dwellings → ornate Merchant estates tells story of civilization development without text
Presence of cloth workshops, alcohol distilleries, tobacco plantations, and spice fields visually indicates which goods a colony specializes in and what trade routes exist
Weapon factories, barracks, and coastal fortifications positioned on map communicate defensive posture and military capability; weapon factories only appear after certain population milestone
Visual flow of trading ships between colonies indicates economic interdependence and successful trade networks without UI explanation
Cluster of enemy-colored structures on adjacent islands visually communicates threat level and competitive pressure without explicit warning
Visual decay of buildings and empty plots communicate population decline and failed colonies without UI messages

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stay in settlements, contribute taxes, allow housing upgrades to next tier; visually remain in place
Emigrate (population decline), riot (visual disturbance), stop paying taxes; reduce building density
Cannot upgrade to Citizen status; remain in basic housing; limit economic growth
Stationed at coastal defenses or settlement perimeters; visually stationary
Mobile across map; engage enemy colonies and units; indicate active warfare state
Move between colonies on established routes; visually indicate economic health and inter-colony dependency
Remain on isolated islands; do not expand; minimal interaction with player colonies
Offer trading transactions; travel to player colonies; represent external economic opportunity
Pace of AI expansion and building scales with player speed; slower players face slower AI opponents; faster players face accelerated AI competition
Generate goods and tax revenue; display active production animations
Cease operation; no visual activity; contribute zero tax revenue

notes: Anno 1602 uses a deliberately nationality-neutral design philosophy: instead of distinct civilizations with unique traits (unlike Age of Empires or The Settlers), all players choose from four colored banners, making visual faction identity purely color-based. The game communicates world-state primarily through: (1) housing architecture tiers reflecting population class progression; (2) building density and diversity indicating economic development stage; (3) ship and trade route visibility showing economic relationships; (4) military structure presence indicating military capability; (5) population UI metrics reflecting satisfaction/crisis. The progressive AI system is notable—gameplay pace itself becomes a world-state signal, with AI opponent activity scaling to player speed. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on spatial layout (island size, proximity), visible infrastructure (production chains), and temporal progression of building types rather than narrative text. Faction identity is minimalist: color-coded banners and unit colors distinguish player from rivals, while neutral factions (natives, traders) have distinct visual architectural styles. Danger and safety signals are economic rather than environmental—population decline, riots, and tax loss indicate danger; social class progression, trade flows, and territory expansion indicate safety.
